description:
Feature ID: -sLzt_DJ In the conversation view one block is a "Thinking" block. Currently it shows as white text in the conversation window. Make the following changes: - Add a 10px padding on top and bottom of the thinking block - Indent the thinking block slightly by using the icon ./images/thinking64x64.png to the let of the thinking text. - Any time you find an english puncation mark followed by a letter and not a space, move to a new line automatically. For example "something.Now" has a period followed by the word Now without a space. So insert a
before Now.
planPoints: 1. Copy thinking64x64.png to web/static/images/ so it's served by the static file handler — copied to web/static/images/thinking64x64.png
2. Update CSS: add 10px top/bottom padding to .conv-thinking, add flexbox layout with icon on the left — added padding, flex layout, .conv-thinking-icon and .conv-thinking-body classes in main.css
3. Update renderThinkingLines() in client.js to insert line breaks when punctuation is immediately followed by a letter — added regex replace after escHtml for .!?;:, followed by a letter
4. Update the _thinking() renderer in client.js to include the thinking icon to the left of the content — added img tag with .conv-thinking-icon class and wrapped content in .conv-thinking-body div
5. Verify all changes work together and are consistent — typecheck passes, CSS and JS changes are compatible
title: Style Thinking blocks with padding, icons, and line breaks
summary: Enhanced the thinking block display in the web conversation by adding 10px vertical padding, positioning a thinking icon to the left of the text using flexbox layout, and automatically inserting line breaks when English punctuation marks are immediately followed by letters without spaces. The changes were implemented across the CSS styles and JavaScript renderers to ensure the thinking blocks are visually distinct and more readable in the conversation view.

conversationHistory.content: I'll start by reading the feature file to understand what needs to be built.

Search the codebase at /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot for:
1. How the conversation view is rendered (look for React components, HTML templates, or UI code that displays conversation messages)
2. Where "thinking" blocks are handled/rendered
3. Any existing CSS/styling for thinking blocks
4. The images directory - confirm thinking64x64.png exists

Be very thorough - check src/, any public/ or static/ directories, and look for keywords like "thinking", "conversation", "message", "block" in the codebase.
Let me find the thinking CSS and check how static images are served.

Grep: {"pattern":"conv-thinking","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/css/main.css","output_mode":"content","-n":true,"-C":3}
Grep: {"pattern":"static|images|express\\.static|serveStatic","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src","output_mode":"content","-n":true,"glob":"*.ts"}
Grep: {"pattern":"WEB_ROOT","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/web/routes.ts","output_mode":"content","-n":true,"head_limit":5}

ls /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/images/
ls /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/

I need to copy the thinking icon to the web static images directory. Now let me also read the `renderThinkingLines` function more carefully.Now I have a full understanding. Let me append the plan to the feature file and then implement.Now let me implement. Step 1: copy the image.
cp /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/images/thinking64x64.png /Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/images/thinking64x64.png
Step 2: Update CSS for padding and flexbox layout with icon.
Step 3: Update renderThinkingLines() to insert line breaks after punctuation followed immediately by a letter.
Step 4: Update the _thinking() renderer to include the icon.
Step 5: Let me check if there's a code-assist preview that also renders thinking blocks, and verify the ca-preview-body override is still consistent.
Grep: {"pattern":"ca-preview-body .conv-thinking","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/css/main.css","output_mode":"content","-n":true,"-C":5}
That override is fine — it just adds font-size and line-height, doesn't conflict with the new flex layout. Let me also verify the build still passes.
npm run typecheck 2>&1 | tail -5

Typecheck passes. Now mark step 5 complete and add the summary.All changes are complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented: 1. **Copied `thinking64x64.png`** to `web/static/images/` so it's accessible via the web server's static file handler. 2. **Updated CSS** (`main.css`): - Added `padding: 10px 0` to `.conv-thinking` for top/bottom spacing - Changed to `display: flex` layout so the icon sits to the left of the text - Added `.conv-thinking-icon` (24×24px, flex-shrink: 0) and `.conv-thinking-body` (flex: 1) classes 3. **Updated `renderThinkingLines()`** (`client.js`): Added a regex `(/([.!?;:,])([A-Za-z])/g)` that inserts a `
` whenever a punctuation mark is immediately followed by a letter with no space. 4. **Updated `_thinking()` renderer** (`client.js`): Wrapped the thinking content in an `` + `
` structure to display the thinking icon to the left of the text.
✅ Feature complete — Cost: $0.7573  Turns: 25  Time: 167.3s

In the "command" type block we should check for the command Grep which appears like this example: Grep: {"pattern":"ca-preview-body .conv-thinking","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/css/main.css","output_mode":"content","-n":true,"-C":5}.    When we find grep we have a pattern, path, output_mode, and some others like -n and -C.   Instead just show the pattern in Path in a nice way like "Grepping for [Pattern] in [Path]"
Let me find the relevant code that handles the "command" type block display.
Grep: {"pattern":"command.*type|type.*command","glob":"*.ts","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
Grep: {"pattern":"Grep|grep","glob":"*.ts","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
Grep: {"pattern":"\"command\"","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/ui/store.ts","output_mode":"content","-C":5}
Now let me see where commands are pushed - specifically where tool use is parsed and the command string is built:
Grep: {"pattern":"pushConversationCommand","output_mode":"files_with_matches"}
Grep: {"pattern":"pushConversationCommand","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/KaiAgent.ts","output_mode":"content","-C":10}
Now I can see the pattern. In the `else` branch (line 244-249), tools like Grep are handled generically. I need to add a specific case for Grep. Let me read the full function for context:
Read: {"file_path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/KaiAgent.ts","offset":243,"limit":10}
Now I'll add a specific handler for Grep before the generic else:
Now let me verify the `basename` import is available since I'm using it for the grep path:
Grep: {"pattern":"import.*basename","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/KaiAgent.ts","output_mode":"content"}
Good, `basename` is already imported. Let me run the typecheck:
Bash: {"command":"npm run typecheck 2>&1"}
Typecheck passes. Let me also run the tests:
Bash: {"command":"npm test 2>&1","timeout":30000}

The only failure is the SDK smoke test due to network restrictions (sandbox), not related to my change. All other tests pass.

Let me also check the snapshot test to make sure Grep routing is tested there:
Grep: {"pattern":"Grep","path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/src/__tests__/uiStoreSnapshots.test.ts","output_mode":"content","-C":3}

No existing Grep-specific tests in the snapshots. The change is clean.

Here's what I did: In `routeToolUse()` in `KaiAgent.ts`, I added a specific handler for the `"Grep"` tool before the generic fallback. When a Grep tool call comes in, instead of dumping the raw JSON like `Grep: {"pattern":"ca-preview-body...","path":"/Users/brian/...",...}`, it now displays a clean label like:

> **Grepping for ca-preview-body .conv-thinking in main.css**

It extracts the `pattern` and uses `basename()` on the `path` (falling back to `"."` if no path is given) for a concise, readable display in both the InkJS command panel and the web conversation feed.
Follow-up complete — Cost: $0.1854  Turns: 12

When a user provides input it is displayed in css conv-user-bubble which has 4 rounded corners and the bottom right corner is a little tail for a chat bubble.  That bottom right corner should not have a rounded radius
Grep: {"pattern":"conv-user-bubble"}
Grep: {"pattern":"conv-user-bubble","path":"web/static/css/main.css","output_mode":"content","-A":10}
The bottom-right corner already has a small radius (`4px`) instead of `0`. Let me set it to `0` to remove the rounding entirely for the tail corner.
Edit: {"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/css/main.css","old_string":"  border-radius: 18px 18px 4px 18px;","new_string":"  border-radius: 18px 18px 0 18px;"}
Read: {"file_path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/css/main.css","offset":700,"limit":15}
Edit: {"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/Users/brian/Projects/KaiBot/web/static/css/main.css","old_string":"  border-radius: 18px 18px 4px 18px;","new_string":"  border-radius: 18px 18px 0 18px;"}
Updated the `.conv-user-bubble` border-radius from `18px 18px 4px 18px` to `18px 18px 0 18px`. The bottom-right corner now has no rounding, creating a sharp edge where the tail meets the bubble.
Follow-up complete — Cost: $0.0891  Turns: 6
